_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning that things around us are made from different materials and each material has its own features.

_source.w: We are learning that things around us are made from different materials and each material has its own features.
y: Knowing about materials helps us understand why different things are made from different stuff.
People choose materials for a reason — like using glass for windows because we can see through it.
Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples have always selected the right natural materials for tools, shelter and art.
eg: Feeling that metal is cold and smooth while wood is warm and rough.
Noticing that glass is see-through but wood is not.
Sorting classroom objects by what they are made from — plastic, metal, wood, fabric.
Discovering that rubber is stretchy and paper tears easily.
Talking about why raincoats are made from waterproof material.
Comparing how a wooden block and a sponge feel different.
